scope: Art and science of structural modeling and design. Novel analytical, computational, and experimental simulation techniques. Proposal and evaluation of new structural systems; study of existing systems. Methods for maintenance, rehabilitation, and monitoring of existing structures. Properties of engineering materials as related to structural behavior. Contributions at the intersection of structural engineering and other disciplines including AI/ML.

manuscript_structure.abstract_note: Single paragraph, plain language, no math or references. Must include purpose, scope, procedures, and major findings.
manuscript_structure.title_note: Should not begin with articles (A, The) or phrases like Analysis of, Theory of
manuscript_structure.keywords_note: Applicable keywords for online search, reviewed against abstract
manuscript_structure.practical_applications: 150-200 words for non-academic audience describing results and potential applications

formatting.footnotes: Not permitted (except Journal of Legal Affairs)

references.format: Author (Year) in text, no comma between name and year
examples: Beskos, D. E. 1987. "Boundary element methods in dynamic analysis." Appl. Mech. Rev., 40 (1), 1-23. https://doi.org/10.1115/1.3149529.
Evans, G. M., and J. C. Furlong. 2003. Environmental biotechnology: Theory and applications. Chichester, UK: Wiley.
ACI (American Concrete Institute). 1989. Building code requirement for reinforced concrete. ACI 318-89. Farmington Hills, MI: ACI.
references.journal_abbreviation_note: Journal and conference titles abbreviated, Title Case. Article titles in quotation marks, sentence case. Book titles in italics.

figures_tables.placement: Submitted as separate files, each on separate page
figures_tables.figure_caption_style: Fig. {n}. Caption text in sentence case.
figures_tables.table_caption_style: Table {n}. Title text in sentence case.
figures_tables.table_rules: No vertical rules. Horizontal rules only at top, below headings, and at bottom.
figures_tables.color_note: Color free online; print color incurs fees. Must be readable in black and white.

submission_notes.units: SI units mandatory as primary. US customary may follow in parentheses.
submission_notes.equations: Numbered sequentially, placed in body text only (never in figures or tables)
submission_notes.math_notation: Italic for variables, bold for vectors/matrices. All symbols in Notation section.
submission_notes.notation_section: Alphabetical: capitals before lowercase, Greek after English, non-alphabetical last. Definitions end with semicolons.
submission_notes.ethical_requirements: Original unpublished work, not under review elsewhere. Gender-neutral language required.
